methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $124M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Quantum Computing (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 7 same-sector peers exist in Quantum Computing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: planqc
private.rationale: Same sector (Quantum Computing), same stage (Series A)

data.narrative:
Quantum Art is a Series A quantum computing company founded by Tal David and Nitzan Akerman that has raised $124M to date. The quantum computing sector comprises 11 comparable companies: 3 public (IonQ, Rigetti Computing, D-Wave Quantum) and 8 private peers including Quantinuum and Infleqtion.

Stage-progression analysis proves the only applicable method here. Using total capital raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A to subsequent-stage transitions yields an implied valuation of $248M. A peer-median approach could not be applied due to insufficient narrow-filter comparables: while the quantum computing universe contains 7 same-sector companies, none satisfied the concurrent filters of matching stage, adjacent stage, or similar valuation tier. Requiring a minimum of three datapoints for statistical relevance, this method was excluded to avoid misleading synthesis.

The triangulation analysis supports a median valuation of $248M with a low bound of $211M and high bound of $285M, reflecting ±15% confidence intervals around the single applicable method. Key uncertainties include the representativeness of the 2.0x multiplier for this specific stage transition within quantum computing, the absence of direct peer benchmarking, and reliance on total raised as a valuation proxy rather than observed market transactions. This range should be treated as indicative pending additional market data or comparable financings.
